role: Low-level geometry library that computes wheel, polar placement, square grid, chord, arrow, wedge, and sector-color math for the seed visualization.
capabilities: Compute wheel center and inscribed-circle radius from viewport
Place polar projection points onto the wheel
Compute concentric ring radii in pixels
Map sector index to an HSL hue using CSS theme variables
Compute directed quadratic-bezier chord paths between sector mid-angles
Compute deterministic square-grid cell centers with sub-cell jitter
Enumerate dyadic nesting division levels with opacity weights
Generate SVG wedge paths for sector wedges
substance_why: The file implements concrete, well-documented geometric functions and mandated constants used to lay out the wheel, square grid, chords, arrowheads, and wedges.
interactions_missing: No event handling, hover, click, or focus wiring
No resize observer or responsive recalculation integration
No animation, transition, or gesture helpers
performance_risks: sectorHue reads getComputedStyle on every call, which can force layout recalculation in hot render loops
No memoization of wheel geometry, ring radii, or repeated trigonometric computations
CSS variable fallback path still queries the DOM even when the value is unused
reuse_verdict_why: The polar/square coordinate math and mandated constants are domain-specific and should be preserved, but SVG string generation and CSS-dependent color logic should be decoupled for the new renderer.
decomposition_hints: Split pure coordinate geometry (wheelGeom, placePolar, gridCellCenter) from SVG path string generation (wedgePath, chordPath, arrowHead)
Split theming/color logic (sectorHue, cssVar) into a dedicated theme module
Isolate deterministic jitter/hash (hash01) so grid placement can choose jitter strategy independently
Add a caching/memoization layer for wheel geometry and expensive DOM reads
